reasoning: WorldAtlas has already updated its dedicated page for the Gulf of Mexico to include 'Gulf of America' in the title, indicating a willingness to adopt the new US-mandated name for SEO or relevance to its US audience. However, the resolution specifically targets the North America page (a potentially legacy URL if not a redirect, which historically featured static maps). Updating site-wide maps or secondary text across all continent pages involves more effort than changing a specific article title. With only about 1.5 months until the July 1 deadline, the likelihood that WorldAtlas updates this specific page's map or text to include 'Gulf of America' is plausible but less than even, given that international consensus remains 'Gulf of Mexico' and graphic updates can be deprioritized.
batch_queries: Will WorldAtlas.com display the body of water northwest of the Caribbean Sea as the Gulf of America before July 1, 2025?
